ooh, wheat has heretofore been
kept cheap in price because it was
raised on the cheap and rich lands
of the pioneer. 1t was always
raised on the frontiers in bonanza
fields and by machinery. The
frontier is now retired into Can
ada and the average crop of wheat
per acre has been lessened from
year to year in this country. The
farmer cannot afford to raise
spring wheat now on old land. He
has done all he could to keep the
price of his products down. He
does all his work by machinery
that he can.


"A short time ago l was back
on the old place that | used to
farm in Iowa. A couple of my
younger rel.ltives now run the
place | found them doing twice
the work in the hay and grain
fields by machinery, with half OF
the help that it required when
ran It,


ITS a fact that New York and
the other large cities are full of
unemployed labor, but it is utterly
impossible to get this labor to go
out and help the farmer. They
will not leave the city, and if they
did they would be found of very
little value, because they know it
all and it seems a waste of time
to try to teach them to be useful
